reasoning_chain_text: 1. For 2022, 2023, and 2024, identify Washington, DC wards in the top 4 by building permits each year and keep the wards that qualify every year.
2. Among those wards, compare crime-incident counts in 2022, 2023, and 2024 and keep the two wards with the highest average crime-incident counts.
3. For the wards from step 2, compare street-sweeping schedule counts in 2022, 2023, and 2024 and return the ward with the highest average schedule count.
original_final_question: First, identify the Washington, DC ward that consistently ranked in the top four for building permits from 2022 to 2024 and had one of the highest crime rates. Among the top candidates, identify the ward that had the most frequent street sweeping schedule. Return the ward only (e.g., `Ward X`). Your response should be in the format [Answer].
